sourceMovie.title: ¿Quieres ser mi novia?
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit ¿Quieres ser mi novia? for the same reason it broke through in 2026: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a romance rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Ihtzi Hurtado keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: To All the Boys I've Loved Before
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: After ¿Quieres ser mi novia?, many fans queue To All the Boys I've Loved Before (2018) next because the pairing feels intuitive. You still get comedy and romance and the same appetite for comic timing and sharper banter, wrapped in a new story that stands on its own.

recommendations.title: The Kissing Booth 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: After ¿Quieres ser mi novia?, many fans queue The Kissing Booth 2 (2020) next because the pairing feels intuitive. You still get comedy and romance and the same appetite for comic timing and sharper banter, wrapped in a new story that stands on its own.

recommendations.title: To All the Boys: P.S. I Still Love You
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: ¿Quieres ser mi novia? and To All the Boys: P.S. I Still Love You (2020) are not the same movie, but they rhyme in the ways that matter. Each commits to comedy and romance, brings comic timing and sharper banter, and trusts the audience to stay with a bold swing.

faq.question: What should I watch after ¿Quieres ser mi novia??
faq.answer: Try To All the Boys I've Loved Before, The Kissing Booth 2, To All the Boys: P.S. I Still Love You—each shares DNA with ¿Quieres ser mi novia? while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is ¿Quieres ser mi novia??
faq.answer: ¿Quieres ser mi novia? (2026) is best described as Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is ¿Quieres ser mi novia? part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "¿Quieres ser mi... ? - Colección" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
